story.text: As the loop, I need a whole BODY of targets — not one dial — that I measure actual-vs-target and feel as pressure, so I can fill a volume of work (opened/closed, merges, owner acts, and more) the way orchestrate already heats and cools one dial, because bdo asked the loop to 'fill a volume based on a setpoint ... the numbers are examples and the named items are suggestions — we define the volume, the expectations, and the rewards and consequences, not the model.'

briefing.headline: The volume scoreboard: the one-dial thermostat widened to the whole body
briefing.value: You asked the loop to fill a volume against a setpoint — a body of targets, not the single step_budget_per_tick dial. This is the sensor: each dimension is an admitted record (name, measure, target, period); the meter scores actual-vs-target and reports under/at/over as pressure. Generative by your rule — the numbers are examples and the dimensions are declared, never coded.
briefing.why_now: orchestrate runs the heat/cool reflex on one dial. To spend agents to fill work the loop needs that same reflex across many dimensions — and a scoreboard that refuses to count a dimension it cannot honestly measure (no-gauge), so pressure is never faked.
briefing.if_accepted: Dimensions are declared by you (--by); the loop reads the meter to pick under-target work; new dimensions add a measure + an admitted target, never a schema change. The slow loop can later infer a resting target per dimension (D2 in the agent-summoning ledger).
briefing.if_rejected: The loop keeps one dial; 'fill a volume' stays prose with no sensor; agent-spend has no scoreboard to aim at.
briefing.cost_of_wrong_call: Low and reversible: volume.py is a read-only fold plus one signed admit verb; it writes only admitted dimension records (superseding, never erasing). No truth moved, no gate logic. Reverting is deleting a module.
briefing.mechanism: MEASURES are small pluggable counters over the log (issues opened/closed/reopened, owner acts, merges, verdicts written) read over a window; read_volume folds the latest admitted volume_dim per name; meter scores actual/target -> under/at/over; a measure with no counter reads no-gauge (never filled). admit declares a dimension, refusing a missing --by and a non-positive target.

source_artifacts: bdo 2026-06-21: 'fill a volume based on a setpoint ... the numbers are examples'
